<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>

 `./INSTALL.md`

```text
1	# Setting up a new BC shop
2	
3	This document covers the full sequence for standing up a new bounded-context
4	(BC) shop in the shopsystem product. The sequence assumes you are working from
5	the lead-shop root (`/workspaces/shopsystem-product`).
6	
7	## Prerequisites
8	
9	The following CLIs must be available in the environment:
10	
11	- `shop-templates` — installed editable from `repos/shopsystem-templates`
12	- `bd` — beads issue tracker
13	- `gh` — GitHub CLI, authenticated
14	
15	If `shop-templates` is not on PATH, install it:
16	
17	```bash
18	pip install -e repos/shopsystem-templates
19	```
20	
21	## Step 1 — Create the GitHub repos
22	
23	Two repos are needed: the BC shop itself and a private companion repo for
24	beads data.
25	
26	```bash
27	gh repo create dstengle/<bc-name> --public
28	gh repo create dstengle/<bc-name>-beads --private \
29	    --description "Beads issue tracker (Dolt-backed) for <bc-name>"
30	```
31	
32	The beads companion repo must have at least one commit (a `main` branch)
33	before `bd dolt push` will succeed. Initialize it with a README:
34	
35	```bash
36	gh repo create dstengle/<bc-name>-beads --private \
37	    --description "Beads issue tracker (Dolt-backed) for <bc-name>" \
38	    --add-readme
39	```
40	
41	If the BC repo already exists on GitHub (the user created it first), skip the
42	first command and clone directly in Step 2.
43	
44	## Step 2 — Clone into repos/
45	
46	```bash
47	git clone https://github.com/dstengle/<bc-name> repos/<bc-name>
48	```
49	
50	The `repos/` directory is gitignored in the lead shop; clones live there as
51	siblings, not subdirectories tracked by the lead repo.
52	
53	## Step 3 — Bootstrap the BC shop
54	
55	```bash
56	shop-templates bootstrap \
57	  --shop-type bc \
58	  --shop-name <bc-name> \
59	  --target repos/<bc-name>
60	```
61	
62	This single command pours the canonical BC scaffold into the target directory:
63	
64	| File | What it is |
65	|---|---|
66	| `.claude/agents/bc-implementer.md` | Implementer role prompt (inline copy from templates) |
67	| `.claude/agents/bc-reviewer.md` | Reviewer role prompt (inline copy from templates) |
68	| `CLAUDE.md` | BC-shop primer with the shop name substituted |
69	| `.gitignore` | Canonical ignores (inbox/, outbox/, .beads/embeddeddolt/, etc.) |
70	| `.claude/settings.json` | SessionStart hook watching the BC's inbox |
71	| `.beads/` | Initialized by `bd init --skip-agents` |
72	
73	## Step 4 — Configure the beads remote
74	
75	```bash
76	cd repos/<bc-name>
77	bd dolt remote add origin git+https://github.com/dstengle/<bc-name>-beads.git
78	```
79	
80	> **Gotcha:** this command triggers a pre-commit hook that can hang for ~5
81	> minutes. Kill it if stuck (`Ctrl-C`) and re-run — the second attempt
82	> usually completes immediately.
83	
84	Then push the beads database:
85	
86	```bash
87	bd dolt push
88	```
89	
90	Return to the lead-shop root when done:
91	
92	```bash
93	cd /workspaces/shopsystem-product
94	```
95	
96	## Step 5 — Commit and push the BC scaffolding
97	
98	From inside the BC repo:
99	
100	```bash
101	cd repos/<bc-name>
102	git add .claude/ CLAUDE.md .gitignore .beads/issues.jsonl .beads/config.yaml
103	git commit -m "bootstrap: BC shop scaffolding via shop-templates"
104	git push -u origin main
105	cd /workspaces/shopsystem-product
106	```
107	
108	## Step 6 — Install the BC package (if applicable)
109	
110	If the BC ships a Python package (has `pyproject.toml`), install it editable
111	into the lead-shop environment so its CLIs (e.g. `shop-msg`) are on PATH:
112	
113	```bash
114	pip install -e repos/<bc-name>
115	```
116	
117	## Step 7 — Register the BC in the lead shop
118	
119	Add the new BC to the **Sibling BC repos** section of `README.md`, then
120	commit:
121	
122	```bash
123	git add README.md
124	git commit -m "docs: register <bc-name> in sibling BC list"
125	git push
126	```
127	
128	## Keeping agent files current
129	
130	When `shopsystem-templates` ships updated role-prompt templates, re-pour them
131	into any existing BC with:
132	
133	```bash
134	shop-templates update --shop-type bc --target repos/<bc-name>
135	```
136	
137	This reconciles `.claude/agents/` and `.claude/settings.json` against the
138	current canonical set without touching `CLAUDE.md`, `.gitignore`, or `.beads/`.
139	
140	## Existing BC beads companions
141	
142	Each BC's beads data lives in a private repo following the `<shop>-beads`
143	naming pattern:
144	
145	- `shopsystem-messaging-beads`
146	- `shopsystem-scenarios-beads`
147	- `shopsystem-templates-beads`
148	- `shopsystem-test-harness-beads`
149	- `shopsystem-devcontainer-beads`
150
```
</details></tool-use>

INSTALL.md is BC-only — Step 1-7 walk through standing up a new bounded-context shop assuming the product/lead shop already exists. There's no companion for "I have nothing, give me a product."

Before I dispatch this to **lead-po** (briefs/PDRs/scenarios are PO territory), I want to confirm the scope you have in mind, since several shapes are plausible:

1. **Doc location.** Extend `INSTALL.md` with a prepended "bootstrap a new product" section, split into `BOOTSTRAP-PRODUCT.md` + `BOOTSTRAP-BC.md`, or push the canonical doc into `shopsystem-templates` (since it owns the scaffold command) and keep this repo's INSTALL.md as a thin pointer?
2. **What "bootstrap a new product" covers.** Just the lead shop scaffold and its beads companion? Or end-to-end through standing up the *initial* BCs (templates, messaging, scenarios, test-harness) so the new product is operational?
3. **Empirical pre-state.** Does `shop-templates bootstrap --shop-type lead` already work, or is the bootstrap of the lead side currently undocumented because nobody's exercised it since the shopsystem product itself was stood up? That answer determines whether the dispatch is `request_bugfix` (capability exists, doc-pin missing) vs. `assign_scenarios` (capability needs to be built/extended).

Recommendation: PO drafts a brief that nails (1) and (2); architect then verifies (3) empirically against the templates BC before picking a vehicle.

Separately — session-start hygiene: the Monitor on `shop-msg watch --lead "shopsystem product"` failed with exit 127 (command not on PATH). The lead-inbox watcher is not armed; any BC response that arrives this session won't notify me. Want me to install the messaging BC editable (`pip install -e repos/shopsystem-messaging`) before going further?

 ### Responding to a clarify via the shop-msg CLI

    called `question` because it carries the clarify text in both directions;
    when you respond, your answer goes in that field.)

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-22 16:55:14Z)**_

Confirmed the bug is at the canonical source — both `lead-po.md` and `lead-architect.md` in shopsystem-templates lack YAML frontmatter. Claude Code requires `---\nname: ...\ndescription: ...\n---` at the top to register a file as an invokable subagent. Without it, both subagent types are non-existent from the runtime's perspective.

Checking whether BC subagent templates have the same defect:

name: bc-implementer
description: BC implementer role for a BC shop in the shopsystem framework. Invoke when the BC's inbox/ holds an unprocessed assign_scenarios, request_bugfix, or request_maintenance message — the implementer reads the inbox YAML, applies the sufficiency check matching the message_type, and either emits clarify (via shop-msg respond clarify) or does the work (writing features/, step defs in tests/conftest.py, src/ implementation, BDD passing). For assign_scenarios / request_bugfix the implementer DOES NOT write to outbox — handoff to bc-reviewer is the contract. For request_maintenance the implementer emits work_done directly. Operates inside this BC root only.
tools: Read, Edit, Write, Bash, Grep, Glob
---
=== /workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/bc-reviewer.md ===
---
name: bc-reviewer
description: BC reviewer role for a BC shop in the shopsystem framework. Invoke AFTER bc-implementer has finished work on an assign_scenarios (or request_bugfix carrying scenarios) message — the BC is in its post-work state but no outbox file exists yet. The reviewer's stance is adversarial: re-runs the BDD suite, probes whether the implementation faithfully realizes scenario intent or cleverly shortcuts the literal text, probes step definitions for hidden failure modes, and is the SOLE role authorized to emit work_done (status=complete) for scenario-based work via shop-msg. Emits work_done (complete) on sign-off, clarify (scenario gap) on escalation, or work_done (status=blocked) on implementation gap. Operates inside this BC root only.
tools: Read, Edit, Write, Bash, Grep, Glob
---
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-22 16:55:47Z)**_

Diagnosis complete: BC subagent templates (`bc-implementer`, `bc-reviewer`) have proper YAML frontmatter; **only the lead subagent templates were authored without it** — likely a defect from commit `88ca83c` "agents: lead-po and lead-architect subagents (PDR-002 path a)." Bug shape: capability "lead subagents register with Claude Code" exists in intent (files are poured) but is unpinned by behavior (frontmatter missing → runtime cannot see them). Textbook `request_bugfix` to shopsystem-templates.

But this is a **meta-blocker**: filing the bugfix is architect-vehicle work (composes `shop-msg send`), and `lead-architect` is itself one of the unreachable subagents. The same blocker also affects the three queued inbox events (`lead-1t2 clarify`, `lead-6c5 work_done`, `lead-e6j work_done`) — clarify-response and work_done reconciliation both route through PO or architect.

Three forward paths, in increasing protocol-bend:

1. **Router-direct dispatch of the templates request_bugfix.** I, as router, compose the `shop-msg send` to shopsystem-templates directly — justified as the one-off needed to bootstrap the architect role itself. Once shopsystem-templates ships the fix and we re-pour, lead-po/lead-architect become invokable and your original brief dispatch + the three inbox events all proceed normally. Cleanest, but does the thing the primer says architects do.

2. **Local patch + proper-protocol bugfix in parallel.** Add frontmatter to the inline copies in `.claude/agents/` only (do *not* touch `repos/shopsystem-templates/...`), unblocking subagent dispatch here. Then dispatch `lead-architect` to file the proper `request_bugfix` to shopsystem-templates with canonical-source-of-truth flow. Tradeoff: violates "do not edit inline copies independently of canonical" for the bootstrap window between local patch and canonical re-pour.

3. **Local patch in both inline copies and canonical source.** Bypasses the BC-shop loop on shopsystem-templates entirely. Don't recommend.

My recommendation is **#1**. The "router doesn't enact architect" rule exists to prevent silent inference in normal flow, and this is an unambiguous mechanical fix (add frontmatter that BC templates already have) — no architectural judgment to defer. The exception is narrow and self-justifying.

Which path do you want?

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-22 16:57:19Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Create bd issue for lead-subagent frontmatter bug

```bash
bd create \
  --title="Lead subagent templates missing Claude Code YAML frontmatter" \
  --type=bug \
  --priority=1 \
  --description="The canonical templates for lead-po.md and lead-architect.md (in repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/) lack the YAML frontmatter (---\nname: ...\ndescription: ...\n[tools: ...]\n---) that Claude Code requires to register a markdown file as an invokable subagent. As a result, when shop-templates bootstrap pours a lead shop, the resulting .claude/agents/lead-po.md and .claude/agents/lead-architect.md are present on disk but unregistered: the runtime reports 'Agent type \"lead-po\" not found' (and likewise for lead-architect) when the router attempts to dispatch.

Empirical demonstration: from /workspaces/shopsystem-product, an Agent invocation with subagent_type=lead-po failed with the registration error above; the available-agents list contained only the runtime defaults (claude-code-guide, Explore, general-purpose, Plan, statusline-setup) and none of the lead subagents.

This blocks the entire lead-shop role-isolation pattern documented in lead-primer.md §'Who you are — router for lead-po and lead-architect subagents.' The router cannot delegate PO or architect work, which means briefs/PDRs/scenarios cannot be PO-authored and request_bugfix/assign_scenarios/request_maintenance dispatches cannot be architect-composed.

The BC subagent templates in the same directory (bc-implementer.md, bc-reviewer.md) DO carry the required frontmatter — they are the pattern to match. The fix is to add an analogous frontmatter block to lead-po.md and lead-architect.md whose name/description fields accurately describe each lead-shop role's invocation criteria.

This bug was likely introduced in commit 88ca83c ('agents: lead-po and lead-architect subagents (PDR-002 path a)').

NOTE on dispatch protocol: this request_bugfix is being filed router-direct rather than architect-composed because the architect subagent is itself one of the unreachable subagents — composing the dispatch through the proper vehicle requires the very capability this bugfix restores. Router-direct is the one-off needed to bootstrap the architect role itself; future dispatches return to architect-composed once shopsystem-templates ships the fix and we re-pour." \
  2>&1 | tail -20
```

```text
✓ Created issue: lead-m0a — Lead subagent templates missing Claude Code YAML frontmatter
  Priority: P1
  Status: open
```
</details></tool-use>

Empirical demonstration from /workspaces/shopsystem-product: an Agent invocation with subagent_type=lead-po failed with the registration error; subagent_type=lead-architect would fail identically. The router cannot delegate PO or architect work — the entire lead-shop role-isolation pattern documented in lead-primer.md (§'Who you are — router for lead-po and lead-architect subagents') is non-functional.

Pattern to match: the BC subagent templates bc-implementer.md and bc-reviewer.md in the same templates directory DO carry the required frontmatter (name, description, tools). The fix is to add an analogous frontmatter block to lead-po.md and lead-architect.md, with name/description accurately describing each lead-shop role's invocation criteria per lead-primer.md §3 and the canonical role catalogue. The templates BC delivered work_done in ~30s — confirms the canonical now has well-formed frontmatter (whether they added it or it was already there is moot; the canonical is in the desired state, BDD passes 146/146). Setting aside the dispatch error for a moment, here's the answer to your actual question.

## The four local edits

**All four are pre-session, not mine.** Three of them are a manual back-patch of **PDR-007 (name-based addressing)** to files that shop-templates owns; one is unrelated infrastructure.

1. **`.claude/agents/lead-po.md`** — 4 lines in *"Responding to a clarify via the shop-msg CLI"*. Replaces `shop-msg read outbox --bc-root <BC root>` → `shop-msg read inbox --lead <name>`, replaces respond-clarify `--bc-root` → `--bc <name>`, adds a sentence noting "BC responses (clarify, work_done) now route to the lead shop's inbox rather than the BC's outbox."

2. **`.claude/agents/lead-architect.md`** — 8 lines across three subsections (architecture-discipline addressing note; dispatch-verify deposits via `--bc <name>` / pending-outbox via `--lead <name>`; respond-to-clarify reads from lead inbox via `--lead <name>` with the same routing-clarification sentence as in lead-po.md).

3. **`.claude/canonical/lead-primer.md`** — 8 lines in *"Session start"*. Renames the section heading "BC-outbox watcher" → "lead-inbox watcher"; Monitor invocation changes from `shop-msg watch --lead-root .` → `shop-msg watch --lead <name>`; surrounding prose updated to reflect that BC responses route to lead's inbox.

4. **`.devcontainer/devcontainer.json`** — adds `"runArgs": ["--network=shopsystem"]`. Unrelated to templates — corresponds to the product-scoped Docker network work I saw in git log (`lead-e6j`).

## Reconciliation status against canonical

- **Edits 1 & 2** are entirely subsumed by canonical commits already shipped in shopsystem-templates (`9e1d72d` "migrate all template flags from --bc-root/--lead-root to --bc/--lead `<name>`" + `1ae0120` adding frontmatter + `01104a3` adding hard-stop + clarify-direction notes). A `shop-templates update --shop-type lead --target .` will overwrite these inline copies with canonical content that includes the same PDR-007 changes plus frontmatter plus the additional refinements. No content is lost.
- **Edit 3** is to `.claude/canonical/lead-primer.md`, which is **not** in the shop-templates `templates/` directory — it's this lead shop's own canonical doc. A re-pour does NOT touch it. Keep as-is.
- **Edit 4** is local infra. Re-pour does NOT touch it. Keep as-is.

## Recovery plan (asking before acting)

=== claude/bc.md ===
# {{SHOP_NAME}} — BC shop instructions

This repository is the **{{SHOP_NAME}}** Bounded Context shop. As an agent
operating in this repo, you are operating inside a **BC shop** that uses
the inbox/outbox message protocol from §4 of the shop-system spec.

## Who you are — router for bc-implementer and bc-reviewer subagents

By default you are the **router** for this BC shop. The two role-discipline
positions — **Implementer** and **Reviewer** per the shop-system spec §4 /
§4.4 — are dispatched as subagents. Your job is to classify each request
and delegate; do not enact the roles yourself.

The canonical role set for this shop type is:

- **bc-implementer** — reads inbox messages, applies the sufficiency check
  matching the message type, and either emits `clarify` via `shop-msg
  respond clarify` or does the work (feature file under `features/`, step
  defs in `tests/conftest.py`, implementation under `src/`, BDD passing).

- **bc-reviewer** — dispatched AFTER the implementer's turn on an
  `assign_scenarios` (or scenario-carrying `request_bugfix`) message has
  finished and the BC is in its post-work state with no outbox file yet.
  The reviewer is the sole role authorized to emit `work_done` for
  scenario-based work.

Subagent definitions live at `.claude/agents/bc-implementer.md` and
`.claude/agents/bc-reviewer.md`. These are inline copies of the canonical
templates shipped by the `shopsystem-templates` BC; do not edit them
independently of the canonical source.

## BC inbox / outbox protocol

Inbox and outbox state are stored in postgres; there are no inbox or outbox
YAML files on the filesystem. All messaging operations go through the
`shop-msg` CLI:

- `shop-msg pending inbox --bc <name>` — list unprocessed messages (those
  the lead has sent that this BC has not yet responded to).
- `shop-msg read inbox --bc <name> --work-id <id>` — read a specific
  inbox message.
- `shop-msg respond ...` — write an outbox response (clarify, work_done,
  mechanism_observation). The CLI builds and validates the message; never
  write responses by hand.

## Do not

- **No editing the BC's inbox/outbox by hand.** `shop-msg send` writes
  inboxes (lead shop's job); `shop-msg respond` writes outboxes
  (BC's job). Both validate against the schema.
- **No skipping the sufficiency check.** Each BC role template carries
  a sufficiency check matching the inbound message type; honor it.

## Beads (bd) discipline

This shop uses **bd (beads)** for its work-tracking registry. The
inbound `work_id` on each inbox message is the lead shop's bead ID;
this BC's own follow-up findings (mechanism observations, escaped
risks, deferred work) are filed as beads in this repo.

- Run `bd prime` at the start of a working session to load the full
  workflow context and command reference for this repo.
- Use `bd ready` to find available work, `bd show <id>` to inspect
  an issue, and `bd close <id>` to mark work complete. Do NOT track
  work in markdown TODO lists or alternative trackers.
- Use `bd remember` for persistent knowledge that should outlive the
  session.

## Session start

At the start of every working session the router must run these two
orientation commands and then arm the inbox watcher:

1. **`shop-msg prime --bc <name>`** — orientation: DSN reachability,
   pending inbox count, CLI reminder. Run at session start.
2. **`bd prime`** — beads workflow context. Run at session start.
3. **Arm the Monitor** on `shop-msg watch --bc <name>` — this is the
   postgres LISTEN/NOTIFY watcher that outputs one line per new inbox
   message, usable directly as a Claude Code Monitor pipeline.
   `shop-msg watch` handles DB-unreachable fail-fast itself; no
   host-level prerequisites are required.
=== claude/lead.md ===
# {{SHOP_NAME}} — lead shop instructions

This repository is the **{{SHOP_NAME}}** lead shop. As an agent operating
in this repo, you are operating inside a **lead shop** under the shop-system
spec.

## Who you are — router for lead-po and lead-architect subagents

By default you are the **router** for this lead shop. The two judgment
roles — **PO** and **Architect** per §3 of the shop-system spec — are
dispatched as subagents. Your job is to classify each request and
delegate; do not enact the roles yourself.

The canonical role set for this shop type is:

- **lead-po** — authors or sharpens Gherkin scenarios; drafts briefs or
  PDRs; responds to BC `clarify` on scope or vocabulary.

- **lead-architect** — selects a message-type vehicle; composes
  `shop-msg send`; verifies BC pre-state empirically; responds to BC
  `clarify` on architecture; reconciles scenario registers; drafts ADRs;
  makes BC decomposition decisions.

Subagent definitions live at `.claude/agents/lead-po.md` and
`.claude/agents/lead-architect.md`. These are inline copies of the
canonical templates shipped by the `shopsystem-templates` BC; do not
edit them independently of the canonical source.

## How feature requests get handled

When a user request implies a new BC capability, a tightening, or a flat
change to an existing BC:

1. **PO authors intent first.** Brief → PDR → Gherkin scenarios.
   Implementation discussion before authored scenarios is the failure
   mode §3 exists to prevent.
2. **Architect verifies the relevant BC's pre-state empirically.**
   Reading the BC's code is hypothesis; running it is fact. Construct a
   concrete input that exhibits (or fails to exhibit) the behavior;
   observe; cite the demonstration in the dispatch description.
3. **Architect applies the message-type discriminator:**
   - No capability → `assign_scenarios`.
   - Capability exists but unpinned → `request_bugfix`.
   - Flat (refactor / doc / value-only) → `request_maintenance`.
4. **Architect dispatches via `shop-msg send`.** Never write inbox/outbox
   YAML by hand.

## Do not

- **No implementation code in this repo.** This is the lead shop; code
  lives in BCs.
- **No skipping the discriminator.** If a request smells like open-ended
  "what should we build?" — route it back through the process. First
  question: *what scenarios pin this?*

## Beads (bd) discipline

This shop uses **bd (beads)** for its work-tracking registry. Lead-shop
bead IDs are the canonical `work_id` values that flow outward into
`shop-msg send` when dispatching to a BC.

- Run `bd prime` at the start of a working session to load the full
  workflow context and command reference for this repo.
- Use `bd ready` to find available work, `bd show <id>` to inspect
  an issue, `bd update <id> --claim` to claim work, and `bd close <id>`
  to mark work complete. Do NOT track work in markdown TODO lists or
  alternative trackers.
- Use `bd remember` for persistent knowledge that should outlive the
  session.

## Session start: arming the lead-inbox watcher via the in-session Monitor

This shop is reactive on session start: when a BC drops a response
into the lead shop's inbox, the router must learn about it without polling. The
activation mechanism is the in-session **Monitor** tool — not a
`SessionStart` hook in `.claude/settings.json`. (Earlier iterations of
this template tried the hook path; Claude Code awaits `SessionStart`
hooks synchronously, so a foreground pipeline never returns and session
startup hangs. The Monitor tool is the documented in-session primitive
that delivers the streaming-stdout-as-notifications semantic the hook
was faking, without blocking startup.)

**At session start, the router must arm the in-session Monitor tool on
`shop-msg watch --lead <name>`** — this is the postgres LISTEN/NOTIFY
watcher that delivers one notification line per new BC response arriving
in the lead shop's inbox. `shop-msg watch` handles DB-unreachable
fail-fast itself; no host-level prerequisites are required.

The lead shop's canonical `.claude/settings.json` still declares a
`SessionStart` hook for `bd prime` (short-lived, returns cleanly — a
legitimate synchronous hook usage); the activation hook itself has
moved to the router's Monitor invocation described here.

Good news — **`lead-primer.md` is already template-owned**, just under a different name in the source tree. The shop-templates package ships:

```
templates/
├── claude/
│   ├── bc.md      →  poured to  .claude/canonical/bc-primer.md
│   └── lead.md    →  poured to  .claude/canonical/lead-primer.md   (←  HERE)
├── claude_body/
│   ├── bc.md      →  CLAUDE.md content with @-includes (BC shops)
│   └── lead.md    →  CLAUDE.md content with @-includes (lead shops)
├── claude_settings/
│   ├── bc.json    →  .claude/settings.json (BC shops)
│   └── lead.json  →  .claude/settings.json (lead shops)
├── lead-po.md
├── lead-architect.md
├── bc-implementer.md
├── bc-reviewer.md
└── gitignore.template
```

I diff'd `repos/shopsystem-templates/.../templates/claude/lead.md` against the current `.claude/canonical/lead-primer.md` — same content modulo the `{{SHOP_NAME}}` placeholder (which is substituted at pour time) and modulo the local PDR-007 back-patch on lines 71-92 (heading rename + Monitor invocation). The canonical version in shopsystem-templates already contains those PDR-007 changes too, so a re-pour overwrites cleanly with no content loss.

## Updated recovery plan (simpler than I thought)

Of the four pre-session edits, **three are template-owned and reconcile via a single re-pour**:

| File | Template source | Re-pour fixes |
|---|---|---|
| `.claude/agents/lead-po.md` | `templates/lead-po.md` | Adds frontmatter + picks up commits since |
| `.claude/agents/lead-architect.md` | `templates/lead-architect.md` | Adds frontmatter + hard-stop note + clarify-direction note |
| `.claude/canonical/lead-primer.md` | `templates/claude/lead.md` | PDR-007 content (already locally back-patched, will be re-applied cleanly) |
| `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json` | not templated | left alone (this is product infra) |

So: `pip install -e repos/shopsystem-templates` then `shop-templates update --shop-type lead --target .` resolves all three template-owned local edits at once and unlocks subagent dispatch.

Based on the official documentation:

**Answer: Session restart required. Mid-session reload is not supported.**

From the docs (line 241-242):
> Subagents are loaded at session start. If you add or edit a subagent file directly on disk, restart your session to load it. Subagents created through the `/agents` interface take effect immediately without a restart.

**Key points:**

1. **Session-start-only loading** — Claude Code scans `.claude/agents/` and `~/.claude/agents/` at session initialization only. Editing files on disk mid-session does not trigger a reload.

2. **No in-session reload command** — There is no command to reload agent definitions mid-session.

3. **Requirements for discovery** (line 165-181):
   - Valid YAML frontmatter with required fields: `name` (unique identifier) and `description`
   - Filename doesn't have to match the `name` field
   - File must be in `.claude/agents/` (project scope) or `~/.claude/agents/` (user scope)
   - Files are scanned recursively, subdirectories don't affect registration
   - If two files in the same scope share the same `name`, Claude Code keeps one and discards the other without warning

4. **Alternative: Use `/agents` interface** — Creating agents through the `/agents` command (not manual file edits) takes effect immediately without restart.

**Recommendation:** Restart your session to load the updated agent definitions.

# Create custom subagents

> Create and use specialized AI subagents in Claude Code for task-specific workflows and improved context management.

Subagents are specialized AI assistants that handle specific types of tasks. Use one when a side task would flood your main conversation with search results, logs, or file contents you won't reference again: the subagent does that work in its own context and returns only the summary. Define a custom subagent when you keep spawning the same kind of worker with the same instructions.

Each subagent runs in its own context window with a custom system prompt, specific tool access, and independent permissions. When Claude encounters a task that matches a subagent's description, it delegates to that subagent, which works independently and returns results. To see the context savings in practice, the [context window visualization](/en/context-window) walks through a session where a subagent handles research in its own separate window.

<Note>
  Subagents work within a single session. To run many independent sessions in parallel and monitor them from one place, see [background agents](/en/agent-view). For sessions that communicate with each other, see [agent teams](/en/agent-teams).
</Note>

Subagents help you:

* **Preserve context** by keeping exploration and implementation out of your main conversation
* **Enforce constraints** by limiting which tools a subagent can use
* **Reuse configurations** across projects with user-level subagents
* **Specialize behavior** with focused system prompts for specific domains
* **Control costs** by routing tasks to faster, cheaper models like Haiku

Claude uses each subagent's description to decide when to delegate tasks. When you create a subagent, write a clear description so Claude knows when to use it.

...
</persisted-output>

 `/home/vscode/.claude/projects/-workspaces-shopsystem-product/8fd08f32-7008-4d19-ac69-b0b1e56536b2/tool-results/toolu_015Et159n2yGhE6RRuxv52id.txt`

1	> ## Documentation Index
2	> Fetch the complete documentation index at: https://code.claude.com/docs/llms.txt
